text: Add src/cli/DeleteRequestCommand.js to implement the thin command-line delete flow on top of the delete client.
goal: Implement apiease read, apiease update, and apiease delete as public CLI commands.
context: Delete needs the same API key resolution precedence and output-mode support used by the other public request commands.
Unlike create and update, delete should not depend on RequestDefinitionFileLoader.
steps: Parse the delete command arguments for request id, API base URL, shop domain, optional API key, and optional JSON output.
Resolve the API key through ApiEaseHomeConfigurationResolver when --api-key is omitted.
Call the delete client and render either a human-readable confirmation or raw JSON output.
constraints: Keep the command focused on deleting a single request resource by id.
Do not require a request definition file or body for delete.
acceptanceCriteria: The command supports apiease delete --request-id <id> --base-url <url> --shop-domain <shop-domain> with optional --api-key <api-key> and --json.
Missing required arguments fail fast with usage-level output and no API call.
Default output clearly reports delete success or failure and JSON mode emits the raw structured client result.
Success exits zero and failures exit non-zero.
